value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: All foreign nationals who need to obtain a short-term visa to Japan and currently reside in Australia, Brazil, Cambodia, Canada, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Taiwan, United Kingdom, or U.S.A. are eligible to apply for a single-entry tourist visa online.

Emergency call services in Japan are available to anyone in Japan, including foreigners, and can be accessed by dialing 110 for police and 119 for fire and ambulance services. These services do not require payment or coins when calling from public phones.
To use Visit Japan Web, travelers need to create an account with an email and password; with this account, they can register up to 10 family members.
Daily room rates at Tokyo hotels averaged 29,565 yen in 2024

Passengers who have no goods subject to duty and/or tax and goods not prohibited or restricted for import can use the green channel. Personal effects and unaccompanied baggage for personal use are free of duty and/or tax within the allowance specified. Any item whose overseas market value is under 10,000 yen is free of duty and/or tax and is not included in the calculation of the total overseas market value of all articles. There is no duty-free allowance for articles having a market value of more than 200,000 yen each or each set.
Icoca card costs 2000 yen including a 500 yen deposit

Various fast food restaurants offer meals for 800 yen or less; lunch sets at inexpensive restaurants cost around 1000 yen; lunch at better restaurants typically costs between 1500 and 3000 yen; meals at department store restaurants typically cost between 1000 and 2500 yen excluding alcoholic drinks
Himeji Castle charges local residents 1,000 yen ($7.01) and visitors from outside the city 2,500 yen ($17.52)
Nanzoin Temple charges foreign visitors 300 yen entrance fee
Some temples and shrines charge small entrance fees of 300-500 yen ($2-4 USD)
The Japan Rail Pass is available only to non-Japanese nationals visiting Japan on a tourist visa (Temporary Visitor status) with a single entry and a duration of either 15 or 90 days.
If a Suica card is not used for 10 years, it will become invalid. If the card has become invalid, users can reactivate the service by following specific steps.
Many hotels in Japan, especially traditional ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Shinjuku district, Tokyo
Many tourist attractions, such as temples in Kyoto, tend to close at 5:00 p.m.
Door-to-door commute time using public transit in Japan is 50-55 minutes

You can use the Suica IC card to pay fares on public transport systems in Japan, including trains, subways, taxis, and buses. To use it, tap the card when entering and exiting transport gates. On buses, tap only when exiting.
In Japanese accommodations such as ryokan and hotels, it is customary to remove your shoes at the entrance or in your room and wear slippers provided inside the facility.
Hearton Hotel Kyoto is located in Kyoto
Japan's four main islands: Hokkaido, Honshu, Shikoku, and Kyushu
At pretty much all restaurants in Japan you will see on the sign with operating hours a 'L.O.' (last order) that is normally between 30 minutes to an hour before closing time.
The cost of eating in Japan per day can range from 3,500 to 6,000 yen for budget travelers, and from 6,000 to 12,000 yen for mid-range travelers.
Saying “itadakimasu” before a meal is a way of expressing gratitude in Japanese dining etiquette, showing respect for the culture and preserving Japanese heritage.

To use Wi-Fi at major international airports in Japan such as Narita, Haneda, Kansai, New Chitose, Centrair, and Fukuoka, travelers can connect to free Wi-Fi services available within the terminals, but the connection method differs by airport, so travelers should check specific airport Wi-Fi instructions before use.
SIM cards can be bought at BIC Camera shops in Japan or ordered online and picked up at Narita, Haneda, or other airports.
Tokyo
eSIM Card Japan prices range from USD 5 to 30 depending on the data plan

When traveling around Japan, it is essential to carry a good mix of Japanese yen bills and coins, as cash is accepted in nearly 100% of situations including rural shops, train stations, and convenience stores. For cash withdrawals, use ATMs located in 7-Eleven stores, Japan Post, or Shinsei Bank, as these are more likely to accept international debit or credit cards. Additionally, currency exchange services at Bic Camera or Yodobashi Camera offer better rates than airport exchanges.
Cash remains the most accepted payment method in Japan, with ATMs at 7-Eleven and Japan Post Bank being especially foreigner-friendly, supporting English menus and international cards.
Travelers should plan ahead and consider using pocket Wi-Fi, a SIM card, or an eSIM during their trip to Japan because free Wi-Fi spots are limited and mobile roaming tends to be expensive.

Many stores and facilities in Japan still only accept cash payments, including small restaurants, rural facilities, temples, shrines, and festival stalls, making prior preparation with cash essential for tourists.
In 2018, cash payments made up 82% of all payments in Japan.
Cash remains the most accepted payment method in Japan, and it is recommended to carry cash as a backup since some locations may not accept credit cards or digital payments.
A deposit is required to buy an IC travel card in Japan.
Memorize or note the MRT stations you should ride and alight from, as walking is key and you may encounter unique things outside tourist areas.
The Japan Rail Pass is available for periods covering 7, 14, or 21 days and allows unlimited use of covered transport during that time.
Collected info for how_to_use from How to Get Around in Japan? Public Transportation Guide - JAL
Collected info for tips from What are the best ways to navigate Japan's public transport system ...
Always carry a prepaid IC card like Suica, Welcome Suica, or ICOCA for convenient travel on trains, buses, ferries, and cable cars.
Shibuya, Tokyo
If you're on a budget, low cost business hotels are a good option; they are bare bones and have small rooms.
Many restaurants and eateries close for a few hours after lunch around 2:00 p.m. and reopen around 6:00 p.m.; restaurants tend to be open an hour longer on weekends
Beat the crowds by visiting attractions early, as many popular attractions in Japan become unbearably crowded by midday.
In Japanese accommodations such as ryokan and hotels, it is customary to remove your shoes at the entrance or before entering the room and wear slippers provided inside.
Accommodations in Japan can quickly book out during peak seasons in popular cities, so travellers are recommended to book well in advance.
Sapporo
Dinner in Japan is usually busiest between 7 to 8 pm, and lunch busiest between 1 to 2 pm.
A 15-minute walk from Osaka Castle, Chuo, Osaka, Osaka Prefecture, Kinki region
Japan doesn’t have a tipping culture – if you leave cash on the table, it’s assumed you did so by accident.

For fine dining, omakase, or popular Michelin-starred restaurants in Japan, making a reservation is highly recommended because many do not accept walk-ins, have very limited seating, and are in high demand.
11:30 a.m. - 6 p.m. (Closed Tuesday)
When the food is served in Japan, it is customary to join your hands in the 'Namaste' gesture and quietly say 'Itadakimasu', which literally means 'I humbly receive'.

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, requiring the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
Count how many times you chew your food and chew for at least 30 seconds before swallowing to promote balanced eating.
Osaka, Osaka Prefecture, Kinki region, Japan
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.
Collected info for tips from Accommodation in Japan
When visiting attractions in Japan, be respectful by not smoking or eating while walking, avoid talking on trains, and keep mobile phones on silent mode.
You can activate your prepaid Japan SIM card as soon as you arrive at your airport terminal in Japan.
Mount Fuji is located in Japan and is the country's highest mountain
Tokyo is the capital and largest city of Japan
Nara is located in Japan and was the first capital of the country
Hiroshima Castle is located in Hiroshima, Japan
Peace Memorial Park is located in Nagasaki, Japan
Spectacles Bridge (Meganebashi) is located in Japan and is nearly 400 years old
Nikko is accessible as a day trip from Tokyo, Japan
Nara Park is located in Nara, Japan and is known for temples and wild deer
